title: Ranil Wickremesinghe should come up with a road map to improve the real sector in the economy and introduce new technology and align Sri Lanka’s economy to the global economy within the shortest time possible.
body_lines: The Ministry of Finance, under the direction of Prime Minister and Finance Minister Ranil Wickremesinghe, known as RW, has hiked main taxes in the country moving halfway back to the tax regime that prevailed prior to 2020. Before this action, the Inland Revenue Services Union, made up of Assistant and Deputy Commissioners, had addressed a memo to RW suggesting measures to be taken to deliver an improved annual tax harvest of Rs. 1,200 billion. The tax measures introduced by the Finance Ministry are short of these proposals. Perhaps in the next round of tax reforms, the Ministry might reckon those proposals and if it does not do, its budgetary position will remain perilous.

The tax cuts offered by the Gotabaya Rajapaksa administration in January 2020 has been a contentious issue. The rationale presented by his policy advisors for these unsolicited tax concessions goes as follows. The previous tax regime introduced by Finance Minister Mangala Samaraweera had planned to attain fiscal consolidation – action taken to reduce the budget deficit to an affordable level and thereby check on debt accumulation – by increasing tax revenue. This is because the administration at that time had felt that attaining that objective by curtailing Government expenditure was considered an impractical strategy. But the administration had paid attention to rationalising Government expenditure as a means of improving the productivity of the sector. Accordingly, in the medium term, tax revenue was to be raised from 11.8% of GDP in 2018 to 15% by 2023.

However, such a high tax level was viewed as an unnecessary burden imposed on real economic activities by Gota’s policy advisors. Hence, instead of seeking to attain fiscal consolidation by increasing revenue, a strategy aiming at curtailing Government expenditure was planned. On the revenue side, it was planned to concentrate on a small number of large taxpayers and recover the tax loss by collecting the maximum from them. The rationale behind this strategy was that when tax rates are reduced, people would pay more taxes leading to an increase in the total tax revenue, a concept proposed by economist Arthur Laffer and now known as Laffer curve strategy.

When this policy was announced Gota’s election manifesto, it appeared to independent economic analysts that it was a recipe for disaster. I took up the issue in December 2019 when the new government was making plans for its introduction and warned in an article in this series that the Government should control the damage before the unconventional stimulus would backfire (available at: https://www.ft.lk/columns/Tax-cuts-Control-the-damage-before-the-unconventional-stimulus-backfires/4-691207). The following is a reproduction of some of the arguments made in this article.

The Cabinet spokesman Dr. Bandula Gunawardena had hailed the proposed tax cut that it will leave more cash in the hands of individuals and businesses enabling them to overcome the economic hardships they had been undergoing for some time. The public at large also welcomed it because they had more cash on hand to spend.

But the Moody’s Rating Services branded the stimulus as ‘credit negative’ implying that it would lead to a downgrading of the country’s present rating of B1/Stable unless it is managed properly. The Fitch rating had warned that the stimulus package which is hailed by Bandula Gunawardena will derail the budget disciplining exercise started by the previous government. A similar view was expressed by IMF’s Sri Lanka country head too. Dr. Indrajit Coomaraswamy who was about to leave the Central Bank at that time had warned that the stimulus package in question should not undermine the debt sustainability exercise introduced by the previous government.

The World Bank’s Chief Economist for South Asia, Hans Timmer, delivering a public lecture at the Central Bank’s Centre for Banking Studies was more polite in expressing his concerns. He said that though there may be reason for fiscal stimulus, there is no space for it in Sri Lanka and the policymakers should strike a proper balance between fiscal stimulus and the need for preventing the overheating of the economy – a situation where the aggregate demand was rising faster than the aggregate supply.

What it means is that if Sri Lanka’s potential growth is low, stimulating the economy either through fiscal policy or through monetary policy would simply increase the demand causing the prices to increase and the exchange rate to fall. Both these outcomes are what the Government wants to prevent from happening at any cost. Immediately, the reaction of the international community was visible in the form of a decline in the market prices of the international sovereign bonds issued by the Government.

I argued that the expectation of increasing revenue by cutting tax rates following the Laffer curve strategy was not relevant to Sri Lanka. Though the Laffer curve was a general law in economics, empirical studies have shown that it would be mostly effective when the tax rates are 70% or above. But Sri Lanka’s marginal tax rate was just 24% at that time and, hence, it had not reached the level at which the taxpayers would be incentivised to pay more taxes when the rates were cut.

Hence, it is not the Laffer curve which may have prompted Sri Lankan authorities to cut tax rates but some other consideration. That is to provide an additional income to the hands of the taxpayers, catch more liable people into the tax net and increase the tax revenue by getting more people to pay taxes. This last goal had been expressly mentioned in the election manifesto. According to press reports, the tax cut is intended to address both the individual taxpayers and the corporate taxpayers. The Government was therefore to lose a significant amount of tax revenue due to the tax cuts. That was estimated in December 2019 at about Rs. 650-680 billion. That was a revenue loss that could not be afforded by the cash-strapped Government. This advice as well as the warnings of others was not heeded to by Gota’s policy advisors. They went ahead with their program and implemented it.

When Sri Lanka was hit by the outbreak of the COVID-19 pandemic in the early part of 2020, due to the loss in revenue, the country had already lost the necessary fiscal space to address it. Expenditure had ballooned increasing the fiscal deficit to unaffordable levels thereby derailing the fiscal consolidation program initiated by the previous government. Given this predicament, in another article published in May 2020, I suggested to the Gotabaya administration that it should consider postponing the costly tax reforms (available at: https://www.ft.lk/columns/Constrained-fiscal-space-for-post-COVID-19-reconstruction-Consider-postponing-costly-tax-reforms/4-700327).

The Government was to use the Central Bank’s unelected money printing power to fill the budgetary gap and it entailed more problems than solutions. That was because when money was issued excessively, it would cause to establish inflationary pressures in the economy. Since the economy was also spiralling downward, it was obvious that it would also be hit by an economic depression. Hence, Gota’s liberal approach of using Central Bank’s money printing power was infecting Sri Lanka’s economy with a new ailment. That was inflationary depression. That ailment characterised by massive unemployment and low incomes amidst rising prices was to sow the seeds of an uncontrollable social and political disorder. Gota’s policy advisors who led the country to this disaster are no more with him. But the prediction made in May 2020 has now come true causing innumerable hardships to Sri Lankan people.

Hence, my suggestion to Gota was as follows: ‘These types of tax reforms should be tried under normal situations. The present COVID-19 environment is not the ideal situation to do so. It has handicapped the government by restricting its freedom to respond to the rising demands made of it by affected individuals and businesses. If the revenue does not flow into the Consolidated Fund, there is no way for the Minister of Finance to withdraw funds from it by issuing a warrant under the Constitution. Even after the expected approval of a formal budget by the new Parliament, the government will continue to suffer from this handicap due to its low revenue base. It will not be able to meet its financial obligations unless it borrows heavily from the local and foreign markets. Both would worsen the country’s debt situation pushing it further down to an inescapable debt trap. This has to be avoided at all costs. Given these circumstances, it will be prudent for the government to postpone its generous tax reforms until the country returns to normalcy’.

The loss in government revenue due to the tax cut has been the Mother of the Present Economic Crisis. Following an ideology presented by a breakaway group from mainstream economists who called themselves Modern Monetary Theorists, Gota’s policy advisors had liberally used the created moneys in the banking sector to finance the budget deficit.

The danger of following this approach has been presented by Indian economist B.R. Shenoy who taught economics in 1940s at the University of Ceylon. In a book published in 2004 under the title ‘Theoretical Vision’, he has made a distinction between voluntary savings and involuntary savings Voluntary savings are real sacrifices made by people by curtailing real consumption and made available for investors for use in real investments. For instance, if a man produces 100 kg of rice and consumes only 80 kg, he makes a voluntary saving of 20 kg of rice in real terms. This is made available to investors to produce, according to Shenoy, higher order goods which economists call investment goods.

These investment goods will produce more consumption goods in the future, increasing the real income of people thus facilitating them to consume more consumption goods. It therefore leads to a change in the economic structure and thereby generation of long-term economic prosperity. Involuntary savings are those savings created out of the artificial credit and deposit creation by banks with the support of money printed by the central bank. These savings are imaginary savings arising from imaginary assets of financial institutions as against the real savings made by people by curtailing real consumption. Shenoy says that at first such imaginary involuntary savings can increase output but cannot sustain it since they are not backed by real production of investment goods. Hence, it leads to disaster. Sri Lanka is presently experiencing that disaster.

With money printing, aggregate demand for consumption goods will increase. Since most of these goods are obtained from abroad, it will increase the import bill eating into the country’s meagre foreign exchange reserves. Sri Lanka had a foreign exchange balance of $ 7.6 billion in December 2019. This has fallen to a negligible level by May 2022. The worst situation has been that Central Bank’s net foreign assets have become negative and they amount to a negative balance of $ 4.4 billion by March 2022. With no foreign exchange available, exchange rate has been in a freefall and Central Bank’s attempt at fixing it at Rs 360 per dollar has created an acute shortage of foreign currencies in formal banking institutions. The corollary has been the development of a lucrative black market for dollars outside the formal banking institutions.

Without foreign exchange, Sri Lanka cannot make any payments. Payment to commercial and bilateral lenders has already been suspended. There is no foreign currency available in the banking system to pay for essential imports. That includes consumption goods like fuel, cooking gas, medicines, foods, and investment goods like raw materials. These shortages have created queues on one side. They have also deprived the industrial sector of necessary raw materials. As a result, As a result, economic activities have been hampered leading to unemployment, income losses, and prolonged depression. Hence, the loss of revenue due to unplanned tax cuts has been the main contributory agent for these economy-wide ailments.

In this background, RW’s tax hike is welcome. It will certainly pass a tax burden on to people. However, the alternative of printing more money to finance the Government expenditure will be more painful due to inflationary pressures that it will bring about. Already, the official inflation rate is running at 39% with a food inflation of 57%. This will further rise, if the Government resorts to money printing to finance the budget. Hence, RW has chosen the lesser evil.

However, his responsibility should not stop there. He should come up with a road map to improve the real sector in the economy. That road map should necessarily contain strategies to introduce new technology and align Sri Lanka’s economy to the global economy within the shortest time possible. Without this, a mere tax hike to rescue the budget will be fruitless.

si.title: රනිල් වික්‍රමසිංහ මහතා ආර්ථිකයේ සැබෑ ක්‍ෂේත්‍රය වැඩිදියුණු කිරීමටත් නව තාක්‍ෂණය හඳුන්වා දීමටත් හැකි කෙටිම කාලය තුළ ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ ආර්ථිකය ගෝලීය ආර්ථිකයට සමගාමී කිරීමටත් මාර්ග සිතියමක් ඉදිරිපත් කළ යුතුය.
body_lines: RW නමින් හැඳින්වෙන අග්‍රාමාත්‍ය සහ මුදල් අමාත්‍ය රනිල් වික්‍රමසිංහ මහතාගේ උපදෙස් පරිදි මුදල් අමාත්‍යාංශය විසින් 2020 ට පෙර පැවති බදු ක්‍රමයට අඩක් යමින් රටේ ප්‍රධාන බදු ඉහළ දමා ඇත. මෙම ක්‍රියාමාර්ගයට පෙර දේශීය ආදායම් සේවා සංගමය , සහකාර සහ නියෝජ්‍ය කොමසාරිස්වරුන්ගෙන් සැදුම්ලත්, RW වෙත සංදේශයක් යොමු කර ඇති අතර, රු. බිලියන 1,200 කි. මුදල් අමාත්‍යාංශය විසින් හඳුන්වා දී ඇති බදු ක්‍රියාමාර්ග මෙම යෝජනාවලින් අඩුය. සමහර විට මීළඟ බදු ප්‍රතිසංස්කරණ වටයේදී අමාත්‍යාංශය එම යෝජනා ගණන් ගත හැකි අතර එසේ නොකළහොත් එහි අයවැය තත්ත්වය අනතුරුදායක වනු ඇත.

2020 ජනවාරි මාසයේදී ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්ෂ පාලනය විසින් ඉදිරිපත් කරන ලද බදු කප්පාදුව විවාදාත්මක කාරණයක් වී තිබේ. මෙම අනවශ්‍ය බදු සහන සඳහා ඔහුගේ ප්‍රතිපත්ති උපදේශකයන් ඉදිරිපත් කරන තර්කය මෙසේය. මුදල් අමාත්‍ය මංගල සමරවීර මහතා විසින් හඳුන්වා දුන් පෙර බදු ක්‍රමය බදු ආදායම වැඩිකර ගනිමින් අයවැය හිඟය දැරිය හැකි මට්ටමකට අඩුකර ගැනීමටත් එමගින් ණය සමුච්චය වීම පාලනය කිරීමටත් ගෙන ඇති ක්‍රියාමාර්ගය - රාජ්‍ය මූල්‍ය ඒකාග්‍රතාවය ලබා ගැනීමට සැලසුම් කර තිබුණි. මක්නිසාද යත් රජයේ වියදම් කපා හැරීමෙන් එම අරමුණ ඉටුකර ගැනීම ප්‍රායෝගික නොවන උපාය මාර්ගයක් ලෙස සලකන බව එවකට පාලනාධිකාරියට හැඟී තිබූ බැවිනි. එහෙත් අංශයේ ඵලදායිතාව ඉහළ නැංවීමේ මාධ්‍යයක් ලෙස රජයේ වියදම් තාර්කික කිරීම කෙරෙහි පරිපාලනය අවධානය යොමු කර තිබුණි. ඒ අනුව මධ්‍ය කාලීනව බදු ආදායම 2018 වසරේ දළ දේශීය නිෂ්පාදිතයෙන් 11.8% සිට 2023 වන විට 15% දක්වා ඉහළ නැංවීමට නියමිතව තිබුණි.

කෙසේ වෙතත්, එවැනි ඉහළ බදු මට්ටමක් ගෝඨාගේ ප්‍රතිපත්ති උපදේශකයින් විසින් සැබෑ ආර්ථික ක්‍රියාකාරකම් මත පැටවූ අනවශ්‍ය බරක් ලෙස සැලකේ. එහෙයින්, ආදායම් වැඩි කර ගනිමින් රාජ්‍ය මූල්‍ය ඒකාග්‍රතාවය ලබා ගැනීම වෙනුවට රජයේ වියදම් කපා හැරීමේ උපාය මාර්ගයක් සැලසුම් කරන ලදී. ආදායම් පැත්තෙන් විශාල බදු ගෙවන්නන් සුළු පිරිසක් වෙත අවධානය යොමු කර ඔවුන්ගෙන් උපරිමය අයකර ගනිමින් බදු පාඩුව අයකර ගැනීමට සැලසුම් කර තිබුණි. මෙම උපාය මාර්ගය පිටුපස ඇති තාර්කිකත්වය වූයේ බදු අනුපාත අඩු කළ විට, මිනිසුන් වැඩිපුර බදු ගෙවනු ඇති අතර එය සමස්ත බදු ආදායමේ වැඩි වීමක් ඇති කරයි, එය ආර්ථික විද්‍යාඥ ආතර් ලාෆර් විසින් යෝජනා කරන ලද සහ දැන් ලෆර් වක්‍ර උපාය මාර්ගය ලෙස හැඳින්වේ.

මෙම ප්‍රතිපත්තිය ගෝඨාගේ මැතිවරණ ප්‍රකාශනය ප්‍රකාශයට පත් කළ විට ස්වාධීන ආර්ථික විශ්ලේෂකයන්ට පෙනී ගියේ එය ව්‍යසනයේ වට්ටෝරුවක් බවයි. 2019 දෙසැම්බරයේ නව රජය එය හඳුන්වාදීම සඳහා සැලසුම් සකස් කරමින් සිටියදී මම මෙම ප්‍රශ්නය භාරගත් අතර සාම්ප්‍රදායික නොවන උත්තේජකය ආපසු හැරවීමට පෙර රජය විසින් හානිය පාලනය කළ යුතු බවට මෙම ලිපි මාලාවේ ලිපියකින් අනතුරු ඇඟවීය (ලබා ගත හැකිය: https://www.ft .lk/columns/Tax-cuts-Control-the-damage-before-the-conventional-stimulus-backfires/4-691207). පහත දැක්වෙන්නේ මෙම ලිපියෙන් ඉදිරිපත් කරන ලද තර්ක කිහිපයක ප්‍රතිනිෂ්පාදනයකි.

යෝජිත බදු අඩු කිරීම තුළින් පුද්ගලයන් සහ ව්‍යාපාර අතේ වැඩි මුදලක් ඉතිරි වන බවත්, එමගින් ඔවුන් කලක් මුහුණ දුන් ආර්ථික දුෂ්කරතා මඟහරවා ගැනීමට හැකිවන බවත් කැබිනට් ප්‍රකාශක ආචාර්ය බන්දුල ගුණවර්ධන මහතා ප්‍රශංසා කළේය. විශාල වශයෙන් මහජනතාව ද එය පිළිගත්තේ ඔවුන් අතේ වියදම් කිරීමට වැඩි මුදලක් තිබූ බැවිනි.

නමුත් Moody's Rating Services විසින් උත්තේජකය 'ණය සෘණ' ලෙස හංවඩු ගැසුවේ එය නිසි ලෙස කළමනාකරණය නොකළහොත් එය රටේ වර්තමාන ශ්‍රේණිගත B1/Stable හි පහත වැටීමකට තුඩු දෙනු ඇති බවයි. බන්දුල ගුණවර්ධන විසින් ප්‍රශංසා කරන ලද උත්තේජක පැකේජය පසුගිය රජය විසින් ආරම්භ කරන ලද අයවැය විනය අභ්‍යාසය අඩාල කරනු ඇතැයි ෆිච් ශ්‍රේණිගත කිරීම අනතුරු අඟවා තිබුණි. ජාත්‍යන්තර මූල්‍ය අරමුදලේ ශ්‍රී ලංකා ප්‍රධානියා ද ඒ හා සමාන අදහසක් ප්‍රකාශ කළේය. අදාළ උත්තේජක පැකේජය මගින් පසුගිය රජය හඳුන්වා දුන් ණය තිරසාරභාවය පිළිබඳ ව්‍යායාමය යටපත් නොකළ යුතු බවට ඒ වන විට මහ බැංකුවෙන් පිටව යාමට සූදානම්ව සිටි ආචාර්ය ඉන්ද්‍රජිත් කුමාරස්වාමි අනතුරු අඟවා තිබුණි.

ලෝක බැංකුවේ දකුණු ආසියාව සඳහා වන ප්‍රධාන ආර්ථික විද්‍යාඥ හාන්ස් ටිමර්, මහ බැංකුවේ බැංකු අධ්‍යයන මධ්‍යස්ථානයේදී ප්‍රසිද්ධ දේශනයක් පවත්වමින් තම උත්සුකයන් ප්‍රකාශ කිරීමේදී වඩාත් ආචාරශීලී විය. රාජ්‍ය මූල්‍ය උත්තේජනයට හේතුවක් තිබිය හැකි නමුත් ශ්‍රී ලංකාව තුළ ඒ සඳහා ඉඩක් නොමැති බවත්, ප්‍රතිපත්ති සම්පාදකයින් විසින් මූල්‍ය උත්තේජක සහ ආර්ථිකයේ උනුසුම් වීම වැලැක්වීමේ අවශ්‍යතාවය අතර නිසි සමතුලිතතාවයක් ඇති කළ යුතු බවත් ඔහු පැවසීය. සමස්ත සැපයුමට වඩා වේගයෙන් ඉහළ යයි.

එහි තේරුම නම්, ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ විභව වර්ධන වේගය අඩු නම්, මූල්‍ය ප්‍රතිපත්ති හරහා හෝ මුදල් ප්‍රතිපත්තිය හරහා ආර්ථිකය උත්තේජනය කිරීමෙන් මිල ඉහළ යාමට සහ විනිමය අනුපාතිකය පහත වැටීමට හේතු වන ඉල්ලුම වැඩි වන බවයි. මෙම ප්‍රතිඵල දෙකම රජයට අවශ්‍ය වන්නේ කුමන හෝ මිලකින් හෝ සිදු වීම වැළැක් වීමටය. වහාම ජාත්‍යන්තර ප්‍රජාවගේ ප්‍රතිචාරය රජය විසින් නිකුත් කරන ලද ජාත්‍යන්තර ස්වෛරීත්ව බැඳුම්කරවල වෙළෙඳ පොළ මිලෙහි පහළ යාමක ස්වරූපයෙන් දිස් විය.

මම තර්ක කළේ Laffer curve Strategy එක අනුගමනය කරමින් බදු අනුපාත අඩු කිරීමෙන් ආදායම් වැඩිකර ගැනීමේ අපේක්ෂාව ලංකාවට අදාළ නොවන බවයි. ලාෆර් වක්‍රය ආර්ථික විද්‍යාවේ සාමාන්‍ය නීතියක් වුවද, ආනුභවික අධ්‍යයනයන් පෙන්වා දී ඇත්තේ බදු අනුපාත 70% හෝ ඊට වැඩි වූ විට එය බොහෝ දුරට ඵලදායී වන බවයි. එහෙත් ඒ වන විට ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ ආන්තික බදු අනුපාතිකය 24%ක් පමණක් වූ අතර, එම නිසා බදු අඩු කළ විට බදු ගෙවන්නන් වැඩිපුර බදු ගෙවීමට පෙළඹෙන මට්ටමට එය ළඟා වී නොතිබුණි.

එබැවින් බදු අනුපාත අඩු කිරීමට ශ්‍රී ලංකා බලධාරීන් පොළඹවා ඇත්තේ ලෆර් වක්‍රය නොව වෙනත් සලකා බැලීමකි. එනම් බදු ගෙවන්නන්ගේ අතට අමතර ආදායමක් ලබාදී, වැඩි වගකීම් සහිත පුද්ගලයන් බදු දැලට හසුකර, වැඩි පිරිසක් බදු ගෙවා බදු ආදායම වැඩි කර ගැනීමයි. මේ අවසන් ඉලක්කය මැතිවරණ ප්‍රතිපත්ති ප්‍රකාශයේ පැහැදිලිව සඳහන් කර තිබුණා. පුවත්පත් වාර්තාවලට අනුව, බදු කප්පාදුව තනි බදු ගෙවන්නන් සහ ආයතනික බදු ගෙවන්නන් යන දෙකම ආමන්ත්‍රණය කිරීමට අදහස් කෙරේ. එබැවින් බදු අඩුකිරීම් හේතුවෙන් රජයට සැලකිය යුතු බදු ආදායමක් අහිමි වීමට නියමිතව තිබුණි. එය 2019 දෙසැම්බර් මාසයේදී රු. බිලියන 650-680. එය මුදල් හිඟ රජයට දරාගත නොහැකි ආදායම් පාඩුවක් විය. මෙම උපදෙස් මෙන්ම අන්‍යයන්ගේ අවවාදද ගෝඨාගේ ප්‍රතිපත්ති උපදේශකයන් ගණන් ගත්තේ නැත. ඔවුන් ඔවුන්ගේ වැඩපිළිවෙළ ඉදිරියට ගෙන ගොස් එය ක්‍රියාත්මක කළා.

2020 මුල් භාගයේදී ශ්‍රී ලංකාව COVID-19 වසංගතයෙන් පීඩාවට පත් වූ විට, ආදායමේ පාඩුව හේතුවෙන්, එය විසඳීමට අවශ්‍ය මූල්‍ය අවකාශය දැනටමත් රටට අහිමි වී තිබුණි. පසුගිය රජය විසින් ආරම්භ කරන ලද රාජ්‍ය මූල්‍ය ඒකාග්‍රතා වැඩපිළිවෙල අඩාල කරමින් රාජ්‍ය මූල්‍ය හිඟය දරාගත නොහැකි මට්ටමට ඉහළ යමින් වියදම් ඉහළ ගොස් තිබුණි. මෙම අසීරු තත්ත්වය හමුවේ, 2020 මැයි මාසයේ පළ වූ තවත් ලිපියකින්, අධික වියදම් සහිත බදු ප්‍රතිසංස්කරණ කල් දැමීම ගැන සලකා බැලිය යුතු බව මම ගෝඨාභය පාලනයට යෝජනා කළෙමි (ලබා ගත හැකිය: https://www.ft.lk/columns/Constrained-fiscal-space-for -පශ්චාත්-COVID-19-ප්‍රතිසංවිධානය-කල්දැමීම-මිල අධික-බදු-ප්‍රතිසංස්කරණ/4-700327).

අයවැය හිඩැස පිරවීම සඳහා රජය විසින් මහ බැංකුවේ තේරී පත් නොවූ මුදල් මුද්‍රණ බලය භාවිතා කිරීමට නියමිතව තිබූ අතර එය විසඳුම්වලට වඩා ගැටලු ඇති කළේය. ඒ මුදල් අධික ලෙස නිකුත් කළ විට එය ආර්ථිකයේ උද්ධමන පීඩනයක් ඇති කිරීමට හේතු වන බැවිනි. ආර්ථිකය ද පහළට ඇදෙමින් තිබූ බැවින්, එය ද ආර්ථික අවපාතයකට ගොදුරු වන බව පැහැදිලිය. එහෙයින්, මහ බැංකුවේ මුදල් මුද්‍රණ බලය භාවිත කිරීමේ ගෝඨාගේ ලිබරල් ප්‍රවේශය ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ ආර්ථිකයට නව ව්‍යසනයකට ගොදුරු විය. ඒ උද්ධමන අවපාතයයි. ඉහළ යන මිල ගණන් මධ්‍යයේ දැවැන්ත විරැකියාවෙන් සහ අඩු ආදායම්වලින් සංලක්ෂිත වූ එම රෝගය පාලනය කළ නොහැකි සමාජ හා දේශපාලන ව්‍යාකූලත්වයක බීජ වැපිරීමයි. මේ ව්‍යසනයට රට ගෙන ගිය ගෝඨාගේ ප්‍රතිපත්ති උපදේශකයන් දැන් ඔහු සමඟ නැත. නමුත් 2020 මැයි මාසයේ කී අනාවැකිය දැන් සැබෑ වී ඇත්තේ ශ්‍රී ලාංකීය ජනතාවට අප්‍රමාණ දුෂ්කරතා ඇති කරමිනි.

එබැවින් මා ගෝඨාට කළ යෝජනාව මෙසේය: ‘මෙවැනි බදු ප්‍රතිසංස්කරණ සාමාන්‍ය තත්ත්වයන් යටතේ අත්හදා බැලිය යුතුය. වර්තමාන COVID-19 පරිසරය එසේ කිරීමට සුදුසුම තත්වයක් නොවේ. බලපෑමට ලක් වූ පුද්ගලයන් සහ ව්‍යාපාරිකයන් විසින් ඉල්ලා සිටින නැගී එන ඉල්ලීම්වලට ප්‍රතිචාර දැක්වීමේ නිදහස සීමා කිරීම මගින් එය රජය ආබාධිත කර ඇත. එම ආදායම ඒකාබද්ධ අරමුදලට ගලා නොයන්නේ නම් ආණ්ඩුක්‍රම ව්‍යවස්ථාව යටතේ වරෙන්තුවක් නිකුත් කර මුදල් ඇමැතිවරයාට එයින් මුදල් ආපසු ගැනීමට ක්‍රමයක් නැත. නව පාර්ලිමේන්තුවෙන් විධිමත් අයවැයක් සඳහා අපේක්ෂිත අනුමැතිය ලැබීමෙන් පසුවද, අඩු ආදායම් පදනම මත රජය මෙම ආබාධයෙන් පෙළෙනු ඇත. දේශීය හා විදේශීය වෙළෙඳපොළෙන් විශාල වශයෙන් ණය ගන්නේ නම් මිස එහි මූල්‍ය බැඳීම් සපුරාලීමට නොහැකි වනු ඇත. මේ දෙකම රටේ ණය තත්ත්වය නරක අතට හැරිය හැකි අතර එය තවදුරටත් ගැලවිය නොහැකි ණය උගුලකට තල්ලු කරනු ඇත. මෙය ඕනෑම වියදමකින් වළක්වා ගත යුතුය. මෙම තත්ත්වයන් හමුවේ රට යථා තත්ත්වයට පත් වන තෙක් රජය සිය ත්‍යාගශීලී බදු ප්‍රතිසංස්කරණ කල් දැමීම නුවණට හුරුය.

බදු අඩු කිරීම නිසා රාජ්‍ය ආදායමේ පාඩුව වත්මන් ආර්ථික අර්බුදයේ මව වී තිබේ. නූතන මූල්‍ය න්‍යායාචාර්යවරුන් යැයි කියාගත් ප්‍රධාන ධාරාවේ ආර්ථික විද්‍යාඥයන්ගෙන් බිඳී ගිය කණ්ඩායමක් විසින් ඉදිරිපත් කරන ලද මතවාදයක් අනුව, ගෝඨාගේ ප්‍රතිපත්ති උපදේශකයන් විසින් අයවැය හිඟය පියවා ගැනීමට බැංකු ක්ෂේත්‍රයේ ඇති කරන ලද මුදල් නොමසුරුව භාවිතා කර ඇත.

මෙම ප්‍රවේශය අනුගමනය කිරීමේ අනතුර ඉන්දීය ආර්ථික විද්‍යාඥ බී.ආර්. 1940 ගණන්වල ලංකා විශ්වවිද්‍යාලයේ ආර්ථික විද්‍යාව ඉගැන්වූ ෂෙනෝයි. ‘න්‍යායික දැක්ම’ නමින් 2004 දී ප්‍රකාශයට පත් කරන ලද පොතක ඔහු ස්වේච්ඡා ඉතුරුම් සහ ස්වේච්ඡා ඉතුරුම් අතර වෙනසක් දක්වා ඇත. නිදසුනක් වශයෙන්, මිනිසෙකු සහල් කිලෝග්‍රෑම් 100 ක් නිෂ්පාදනය කර කිලෝග්‍රෑම් 80 ක් පමණක් පරිභෝජනය කරන්නේ නම්, ඔහු ස්වේච්ඡාවෙන් සහල් කිලෝග්‍රෑම් 20 ක් සැබෑ ලෙස ඉතිරි කරයි. ආර්ථික විද්‍යාඥයින් ආයෝජන භාණ්ඩ ලෙස හඳුන්වන ෂෙනෝයිට අනුව මෙය ආයෝජකයින්ට නිෂ්පාදනය කිරීමට ලබා දී ඇත.

මෙම ආයෝජන භාණ්ඩ අනාගතයේ දී වැඩි පරිභෝජන භාණ්ඩ නිෂ්පාදනය කරනු ඇති අතර, මිනිසුන්ගේ සැබෑ ආදායම වැඩි කරන අතර එමඟින් ඔවුන්ට වැඩි පරිභෝජන භාණ්ඩ පරිභෝජනය කිරීමට පහසුකම් සපයයි. එබැවින් එය ආර්ථික ව්‍යුහයේ වෙනසක් ඇති කිරීමටත් එමගින් දිගුකාලීන ආර්ථික සමෘද්ධිය ඇති කිරීමටත් හේතු වේ. ස්වේච්ඡා ඉතුරුම් යනු මහ බැංකුව විසින් මුද්‍රණය කරන ලද මුදල් ආධාරයෙන් බැංකු විසින් කෘතිම ණය සහ තැන්පතු නිර්මාණය කිරීමෙන් නිර්මාණය කරන ලද ඉතුරුම් ය. මෙම ඉතුරුම් යනු සැබෑ පරිභෝජනය සීමා කිරීමෙන් මිනිසුන් කරන සැබෑ ඉතුරුම්වලට පටහැනිව මූල්‍ය ආයතනවල පරිකල්පනීය වත්කම්වලින් පැන නගින කල්පිත ඉතුරුම් වේ. ෂෙනෝයි පවසන්නේ මුලදී එවැනි මනඃකල්පිත ස්වේච්ඡා ඉතිරිකිරීම් මඟින් නිමැවුම වැඩි කළ හැකි නමුත් ආයෝජන භාණ්ඩවල සැබෑ නිෂ්පාදනයෙන් ඒවාට පිටුබලයක් නොමැති බැවින් එය පවත්වා ගත නොහැකි බවයි. එබැවින් එය ව්යසනයට තුඩු දෙයි. ශ්‍රී ලංකාව මේ වන විට එම ව්‍යසනය අත්විඳිමින් සිටී.

මුදල් මුද්‍රණයත් සමඟ පරිභෝජන භාණ්ඩ සඳහා ඇති ඉල්ලුම වැඩිවේ. මෙම භාණ්ඩ බොහොමයක් පිටරටින් ලබා ගන්නා බැවින්, එය රටේ සොච්චම් විදේශ විනිමය සංචිතයට කමින් ආනයන බිල වැඩි කරනු ඇත. 2019 දෙසැම්බර් මාසයේදී ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ විදේශ විනිමය ශේෂය ඩොලර් බිලියන 7.6 ක් විය. මෙය 2022 මැයි වන විට නොසැලකිය හැකි මට්ටමකට වැටී ඇත. නරකම තත්ත්වය වන්නේ මහ බැංකුවේ ශුද්ධ විදේශ වත්කම් සෘණාත්මක වී ඒවා ඩොලර් 4.4 ක සෘණ ශේෂයක් වීමයි. 2022 මාර්තු වනවිට බිලියන. එහි ප්‍රතිඵලය වන්නේ විධිමත් බැංකු ආයතනවලින් පිටත ඩොලර් සඳහා ලාභදායී කළු වෙළෙඳපොළක් වර්ධනය කිරීමයි.

විදේශ විනිමය නොමැතිව ශ්‍රී ලංකාවට කිසිදු ගෙවීමක් කළ නොහැක. වාණිජ සහ ද්විපාර්ශ්වික ණය දෙන්නන්ට ගෙවීම් දැනටමත් අත්හිටුවා ඇත. අත්‍යවශ්‍ය ආනයන සඳහා ගෙවීමට බැංකු පද්ධතිය තුළ විදේශ මුදල් නොමැත. එයට ඉන්ධන, ඉවුම් පිහුම් ගෑස්, ඖෂධ, ආහාර, සහ අමුද්‍රව්‍ය වැනි ආයෝජන භාණ්ඩ වැනි පරිභෝජන භාණ්ඩ ඇතුළත් වේ. මේ හිඟය නිසා එක පැත්තකින් පෝලිම් නිර්මාණය වෙලා. ඔවුන් කර්මාන්ත අංශයට අවශ්‍ය අමුද්‍රව්‍ය ද අහිමි කර ඇත. එහි ප්‍රතිඵලයක් ලෙස ආර්ථික ක්‍රියාකාරකම් අඩාල වී විරැකියාව, ආදායම් අහිමිවීම් සහ දීර්ඝ කාලීන අවපාතයට හේතු වී ඇත. එබැවින්, සැලසුම් නොකළ බදු අඩුකිරීම් හේතුවෙන් ආදායම් අහිමිවීම මෙම ආර්ථිකය පුරා ව්‍යාප්ත වන රෝග සඳහා ප්‍රධාන දායක කාරකය වී ඇත.

මෙම පසුබිම තුළ, RW හි බදු වැඩි කිරීම සාදරයෙන් පිළිගනිමු. එය නිසැකවම ජනතාව මත බදු බරක් පටවනු ඇත. කෙසේ වෙතත්, රජයේ වියදම් පියවා ගැනීම සඳහා වැඩිපුර මුදල් මුද්‍රණය කිරීමේ විකල්පය එය ගෙන එන උද්ධමන පීඩනය හේතුවෙන් වඩාත් වේදනාකාරී වනු ඇත. දැනටමත් නිල උද්ධමන අනුපාතය 39% ක් වන අතර ආහාර උද්ධමනය 57% කි. අයවැයට මුදල් සැපයීම සඳහා රජය මුදල් මුද්‍රණයට යොමු වුවහොත් මෙය තවත් ඉහළ යනු ඇත. එබැවින්, RW තෝරාගෙන ඇත්තේ අඩු නපුරයි.

කෙසේ වෙතත්, ඔහුගේ වගකීම එතැනින් නතර නොවිය යුතුය. ඔහු ආර්ථිකයේ සැබෑ අංශය වැඩිදියුණු කිරීමට මාර්ග සිතියමක් ඉදිරිපත් කළ යුතුය. එම මාර්ග සිතියමේ අවශ්‍යයෙන්ම නව තාක්‍ෂණය හඳුන්වා දීමටත් හැකි කෙටිම කාලය තුළ ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ ආර්ථිකය ගෝලීය ආර්ථිකයට සමගාමී කිරීමටත් උපාය මාර්ග අඩංගු විය යුතුය. එසේ නොමැතිව අයවැය බේරාගැනීම සඳහා හුදු බදු වැඩිකිරීමක් ඵල රහිත වනු ඇත.

ta.title: ரணில் விக்கிரமசிங்க பொருளாதாரத்தில் உண்மையான துறையை மேம்படுத்துவதற்கும் புதிய தொழில்நுட்பத்தை அறிமுகப்படுத்துவதற்கும் குறுகிய காலத்திற்குள் இலங்கையின் பொருளாதாரத்தை உலகளாவிய பொருளாதாரத்துடன் சீரமைப்பதற்கும் ஒரு வரைபடத்தை உருவாக்க வேண்டும்.
body_lines: RW என அழைக்கப்படும் பிரதமர் மற்றும் நிதியமைச்சர் ரணில் விக்ரமசிங்கவின் வழிகாட்டுதலின் கீழ், நிதியமைச்சகம், 2020 க்கு முன்னர் நிலவிய வரி விதிப்புக்கு பாதியிலேயே நாட்டின் பிரதான வரிகளை உயர்த்தியுள்ளது. இந்த நடவடிக்கைக்கு முன்னர், உள்நாட்டு வருமான சேவைகள் சங்கம் , உதவி மற்றும் துணை ஆணையர்களை உள்ளடக்கிய, RW க்கு ஒரு குறிப்பாணை அனுப்பியது, இது மேம்படுத்தப்பட்ட வருடாந்திர வரி அறுவடையை ரூ. 1,200 பில்லியன். நிதி அமைச்சகத்தால் அறிமுகப்படுத்தப்பட்ட வரி நடவடிக்கைகள் இந்த முன்மொழிவுகளில் குறைவு. ஒருவேளை அடுத்த சுற்று வரி சீர்திருத்தங்களில், அமைச்சகம் அந்த முன்மொழிவுகளை கணக்கிடலாம் மற்றும் அவ்வாறு செய்யாவிட்டால், அதன் பட்ஜெட் நிலை ஆபத்தானதாக இருக்கும்.

2020 ஜனவரியில் கோட்டாபய ராஜபக்ச நிர்வாகம் வழங்கிய வரிக் குறைப்பு சர்ச்சைக்குரிய பிரச்சினையாக உள்ளது. இந்த கோரப்படாத வரிச்சலுகைகளுக்கு அவரது கொள்கை ஆலோசகர்கள் முன்வைக்கும் நியாயம் பின்வருமாறு. நிதியமைச்சர் மங்கள சமரவீரவினால் அறிமுகப்படுத்தப்பட்ட முன்னைய வரி விதிப்பு முறையானது, வரி வருவாயை அதிகரிப்பதன் மூலம், வரவு-செலவுத் திட்டப் பற்றாக்குறையை கட்டுப்படியாகக்கூடிய அளவிற்குக் குறைப்பதற்கும் அதன் மூலம் கடன் குவிப்பைக் கட்டுப்படுத்துவதற்கும் எடுக்கப்பட்ட நடவடிக்கை - நிதி ஒருங்கிணைப்பை அடைய திட்டமிட்டிருந்தது. ஏனென்றால், அரசின் செலவினங்களைக் குறைப்பதன் மூலம் அந்த நோக்கத்தை அடைவது நடைமுறைச் சாத்தியமற்ற உத்தியாகக் கருதப்பட்டது என்று அப்போதைய நிர்வாகம் கருதியது. ஆனால் துறையின் உற்பத்தித்திறனை மேம்படுத்துவதற்கான வழிமுறையாக அரசு செலவினங்களை பகுத்தறிவு செய்வதில் நிர்வாகம் கவனம் செலுத்தியது. அதன்படி, நடுத்தர காலத்தில், வரி வருவாய் 2018 இல் மொத்த உள்நாட்டு உற்பத்தியில் 11.8% லிருந்து 2023 க்குள் 15% ஆக உயர்த்தப்பட வேண்டும்.

எவ்வாறாயினும், இத்தகைய உயர் வரி அளவு கோட்டாவின் கொள்கை ஆலோசகர்களால் உண்மையான பொருளாதார நடவடிக்கைகளின் மீது சுமத்தப்பட்ட தேவையற்ற சுமையாக பார்க்கப்பட்டது. எனவே, வருவாயை அதிகரிப்பதன் மூலம் நிதி ஒருங்கிணைப்பை அடைவதற்குப் பதிலாக, அரசாங்க செலவினங்களைக் குறைக்கும் நோக்கத்தில் ஒரு உத்தி திட்டமிடப்பட்டது. வருவாய் தரப்பில், குறைந்த எண்ணிக்கையிலான பெரிய வரி செலுத்துவோர் மீது கவனம் செலுத்தி, அவர்களிடமிருந்து அதிகபட்சமாக வசூலித்து வரி இழப்பை வசூலிக்க திட்டமிடப்பட்டது. இந்த மூலோபாயத்தின் பின்னணியில் உள்ள காரணம் என்னவென்றால், வரி விகிதங்கள் குறைக்கப்படும்போது, ​​மக்கள் அதிக வரிகளை செலுத்துவார்கள், இது மொத்த வரி வருவாயில் அதிகரிப்புக்கு வழிவகுக்கும், இது பொருளாதார நிபுணர் ஆர்தர் லாஃபரால் முன்மொழியப்பட்டது மற்றும் இப்போது லாஃபர் வளைவு உத்தி என்று அழைக்கப்படுகிறது.

இந்தக் கொள்கை கோட்டாவின் தேர்தல் விஞ்ஞாபனமாக அறிவிக்கப்பட்டபோது, ​​அது பேரழிவுக்கான செய்முறை என்று சுதந்திரப் பொருளாதார ஆய்வாளர்களுக்குத் தோன்றியது. 2019 டிசம்பரில், புதிய அரசாங்கம் அதன் அறிமுகத்திற்கான திட்டங்களைத் தயாரித்துக் கொண்டிருந்தபோது, ​​இந்தப் பிரச்சினையை நான் எடுத்துக்கொண்டேன், வழக்கத்திற்கு மாறான தூண்டுதல்கள் பின்வாங்கும் முன், சேதத்தை அரசாங்கம் கட்டுப்படுத்த வேண்டும் என்று இந்தத் தொடரில் ஒரு கட்டுரையில் எச்சரித்தேன் (கிடைப்பது: https://www.ft .lk/columns/Tax-cuts-Control-the-damage-before-the-unconventional-stimulus-backfires/4-691207). இந்தக் கட்டுரையில் முன்வைக்கப்பட்ட சில வாதங்களின் மறுஉருவாக்கம் பின்வருமாறு.

உத்தேச வரிக் குறைப்பு, தனிநபர்கள் மற்றும் வர்த்தகர்களின் கைகளில் அதிக பணத்தை விட்டுச் செல்லும் என்று அமைச்சரவைப் பேச்சாளர் கலாநிதி பந்துல குணவர்தன அவர்கள் சில காலமாக அனுபவித்து வந்த பொருளாதார நெருக்கடிகளை சமாளிக்க உதவும் என்று பாராட்டினார். செலவழிக்க கையில் அதிக பணம் இருந்ததால் பொதுமக்களும் இதை வரவேற்றனர்.

ஆனால் மூடிஸ் ரேட்டிங் சர்வீசஸ் இந்த ஊக்கத்தை 'கிரெடிட் நெகட்டிவ்' என்று முத்திரை குத்தியது, இது நாட்டின் தற்போதைய மதிப்பீடான பி1/நிலையான மதிப்பீட்டை சரியாக நிர்வகிக்காத பட்சத்தில் அது தரமிறக்கப்படும் என்பதைக் குறிக்கிறது. பந்துல குணவர்தனவால் பாராட்டப்பட்ட ஊக்குவிப்புப் பொதி கடந்த அரசாங்கத்தினால் ஆரம்பிக்கப்பட்ட வரவு செலவுத் திட்ட ஒழுங்குமுறைப் பயிற்சியைத் தடம் புரளச் செய்யும் என Fitch மதிப்பீடு எச்சரித்திருந்தது. இதே கருத்தை சர்வதேச நாணய நிதியத்தின் இலங்கை நாட்டுத் தலைவரும் வெளிப்படுத்தினார். குறித்த ஊக்குவிப்புப் பொதியானது கடந்த அரசாங்கத்தினால் அறிமுகப்படுத்தப்பட்ட கடனை நிலைநிறுத்தும் செயற்பாடுகளை சீர்குலைக்கக் கூடாது என அப்போது மத்திய வங்கியிலிருந்து வெளியேறவிருந்த கலாநிதி இந்திரஜித் குமாரசுவாமி எச்சரித்திருந்தார்.

உலக வங்கியின் தெற்காசியாவிற்கான தலைமைப் பொருளாதார நிபுணர் ஹான்ஸ் டிம்மர், மத்திய வங்கியின் வங்கியியல் ஆய்வு மையத்தில் பொது விரிவுரை ஆற்றியபோது, ​​தனது கவலைகளை மிகவும் கண்ணியமாக வெளிப்படுத்தினார். நிதி ஊக்குவிப்புக்கு காரணம் இருந்தாலும், இலங்கையில் அதற்கு இடமில்லை என்றும், கொள்கை வகுப்பாளர்கள் நிதி ஊக்குவிப்பு மற்றும் பொருளாதாரம் சூடுபிடிப்பதைத் தடுப்பதன் அவசியத்திற்கு இடையே சரியான சமநிலையை ஏற்படுத்த வேண்டும் என்றும் அவர் கூறினார். மொத்த விநியோகத்தை விட வேகமாக உயர்கிறது.

இதன் பொருள் என்னவென்றால், இலங்கையின் சாத்தியமான வளர்ச்சி குறைவாக இருந்தால், நிதிக் கொள்கை மூலமாகவோ அல்லது நாணயக் கொள்கை மூலமாகவோ பொருளாதாரத்தைத் தூண்டுவது வெறுமனே தேவையை அதிகரித்து விலைகளை அதிகரிக்கச் செய்து பரிமாற்ற வீதத்தைக் குறைக்கும். இந்த இரண்டு முடிவுகளும் எந்த விலையிலும் நடக்காமல் தடுக்க அரசு விரும்புகிறது. உடனடியாக, சர்வதேச சமூகத்தின் எதிர்வினை, அரசாங்கத்தால் வெளியிடப்பட்ட சர்வதேச இறையாண்மைப் பத்திரங்களின் சந்தை விலைகளில் சரிவு வடிவத்தில் காணப்பட்டது.

லாஃபர் வளைவு மூலோபாயத்தைப் பின்பற்றி வரி விகிதங்களைக் குறைப்பதன் மூலம் வருவாயை அதிகரிக்கும் என்ற எதிர்பார்ப்பு இலங்கைக்கு பொருந்தாது என்று நான் வாதிட்டேன். லாஃபர் வளைவு என்பது பொருளாதாரத்தில் ஒரு பொதுவான சட்டமாக இருந்தாலும், வரி விகிதங்கள் 70% அல்லது அதற்கு மேல் இருக்கும் போது அது மிகவும் பயனுள்ளதாக இருக்கும் என்று அனுபவ ஆய்வுகள் காட்டுகின்றன. ஆனால் அந்த நேரத்தில் இலங்கையின் விளிம்பு வரி விகிதம் வெறும் 24% ஆக இருந்தது, எனவே, வரி செலுத்துவோர் விகிதங்கள் குறைக்கப்படும் போது அதிக வரிகளை செலுத்த ஊக்குவிக்கப்படும் நிலையை எட்டவில்லை.

எனவே, வரி விகிதங்களைக் குறைக்க இலங்கை அதிகாரிகளைத் தூண்டியது லாஃபர் வளைவு அல்ல, ஆனால் வேறு சில கருத்தில் கொள்ள வேண்டும். அதாவது, வரி செலுத்துவோரின் கைகளுக்கு கூடுதல் வருமானத்தை வழங்குவது, அதிக பொறுப்புள்ள நபர்களை வரி வலையில் சிக்க வைப்பது மற்றும் அதிக மக்களை வரி செலுத்துவதன் மூலம் வரி வருவாயை அதிகரிப்பது. இந்தக் கடைசி இலக்கு தேர்தல் விஞ்ஞாபனத்தில் வெளிப்படையாகக் குறிப்பிடப்பட்டிருந்தது. பத்திரிகை செய்திகளின்படி, வரி குறைப்பு தனிப்பட்ட வரி செலுத்துவோர் மற்றும் கார்ப்பரேட் வரி செலுத்துவோர் இருவரையும் நிவர்த்தி செய்யும் நோக்கம் கொண்டது. எனவே வரிக் குறைப்புகளால் அரசாங்கம் கணிசமான அளவு வரி வருவாயை இழக்க நேரிட்டது. இது டிசம்பர் 2019 இல் சுமார் ரூ. 650-680 பில்லியன். பணப்புழக்கம் இல்லாத அரசால் ஈடுசெய்ய முடியாத வருவாய் இழப்பு. இந்த ஆலோசனையும் மற்றவர்களின் எச்சரிக்கைகளும் கோட்டாவின் கொள்கை ஆலோசகர்களால் கவனிக்கப்படவில்லை. அவர்கள் தங்கள் திட்டத்தை முன்னெடுத்துச் சென்று அதைச் செயல்படுத்தினர்.

2020 இன் முற்பகுதியில் COVID-19 தொற்றுநோயால் இலங்கை பாதிக்கப்பட்டபோது, ​​​​வருவாய் இழப்பு காரணமாக, அதை நிவர்த்தி செய்ய தேவையான நிதி இடத்தை நாடு ஏற்கனவே இழந்திருந்தது. செலவினங்கள் பெருகியதால் நிதிப் பற்றாக்குறையை கட்டுப்படியாக முடியாத அளவுக்கு அதிகரித்தது, இதன் மூலம் முந்தைய அரசாங்கத்தால் தொடங்கப்பட்ட நிதி ஒருங்கிணைப்புத் திட்டம் தடம் புரண்டது. இந்த இக்கட்டான சூழ்நிலையில், மே 2020 இல் வெளியிடப்பட்ட மற்றொரு கட்டுரையில், விலையுயர்ந்த வரி சீர்திருத்தங்களை ஒத்திவைப்பது குறித்து பரிசீலிக்க வேண்டும் என்று கோத்தபய நிர்வாகத்திற்கு பரிந்துரைத்தேன் (கிடைக்க: https://www.ft.lk/columns/Constrained-fiscal-space-for -post-COVID-19-reconstruction-consider-postponing-costly-tax-reforms/4-700327).

வரவு செலவுத் திட்ட இடைவெளியை நிரப்புவதற்கு அரசாங்கம் மத்திய வங்கியின் தெரிவுசெய்யப்படாத பணத்தை அச்சடிக்கும் சக்தியைப் பயன்படுத்த வேண்டியிருந்தது. ஏனென்றால், பணம் அதிகமாக வெளியிடப்பட்டால், அது பொருளாதாரத்தில் பணவீக்க அழுத்தத்தை ஏற்படுத்திவிடும். பொருளாதாரமும் கீழ்நோக்கிச் சுழன்று கொண்டிருந்ததால், அதுவும் பொருளாதார மந்தநிலையால் பாதிக்கப்படும் என்பது தெளிவாகத் தெரிந்தது. எனவே, மத்திய வங்கியின் பணத்தை அச்சிடும் ஆற்றலைப் பயன்படுத்தும் கோட்டாவின் தாராளவாத அணுகுமுறை இலங்கையின் பொருளாதாரத்தை ஒரு புதிய நோயால் பாதித்தது. அது பணவீக்க மந்தநிலை. பாரிய வேலையின்மை மற்றும் விலைவாசி உயர்வுக்கு மத்தியில் குறைந்த வருமானம் ஆகியவற்றால் வகைப்படுத்தப்பட்ட அந்த நோய், கட்டுப்படுத்த முடியாத சமூக மற்றும் அரசியல் சீர்கேட்டை விதைப்பதாகும். இந்த பேரழிவிற்கு நாட்டை வழிநடத்திய கோட்டாவின் கொள்கை ஆலோசகர்கள் இப்போது அவருடன் இல்லை. ஆனால் 2020 மே மாதம் கூறப்பட்ட கணிப்பு தற்போது உண்மையாகி இலங்கை மக்களுக்கு எண்ணிலடங்கா கஷ்டங்களை ஏற்படுத்தியுள்ளது.

எனவே, கோட்டாவுக்கு எனது பரிந்துரை பின்வருமாறு: 'இந்த வகையான வரி சீர்திருத்தங்கள் சாதாரண சூழ்நிலையில் முயற்சிக்கப்பட வேண்டும். தற்போதைய கோவிட்-19 சூழல் அவ்வாறு செய்வதற்கு உகந்த சூழ்நிலை அல்ல. பாதிக்கப்பட்ட தனிநபர்கள் மற்றும் வணிகங்களால் எழுப்பப்படும் கோரிக்கைகளுக்கு பதிலளிக்கும் சுதந்திரத்தை கட்டுப்படுத்துவதன் மூலம் அரசாங்கத்தை முடக்கியுள்ளது. வருமானம் ஒருங்கிணைக்கப்பட்ட நிதியில் சேரவில்லை என்றால், அரசமைப்புச் சட்டத்தின்படி பிடியாணை பிறப்பித்து நிதி அமைச்சருக்கு அதில் இருந்து நிதி திரும்பப் பெற வழியில்லை. புதிய பாராளுமன்றத்தின் முறையான வரவு செலவுத் திட்டத்திற்கு எதிர்பார்க்கப்படும் ஒப்புதலுக்குப் பிறகும், அரசாங்கம் அதன் குறைந்த வருவாய் அடிப்படையால் இந்த ஊனத்தால் தொடர்ந்து பாதிக்கப்படும். உள்ளூர் மற்றும் வெளிநாட்டு சந்தைகளில் இருந்து அதிக அளவில் கடன் வாங்கும் வரை அதன் நிதிக் கடமைகளை அது பூர்த்தி செய்ய முடியாது. இரண்டுமே நாட்டின் கடன் நிலைமையை மேலும் மோசமாக்கும், மேலும் தவிர்க்க முடியாத கடன் பொறிக்கு தள்ளும். இது எல்லா விலையிலும் தவிர்க்கப்பட வேண்டும். இந்த சூழ்நிலையில், நாடு இயல்பு நிலைக்கு திரும்பும் வரை அரசாங்கம் தனது தாராளமான வரி சீர்திருத்தங்களை ஒத்திவைப்பது விவேகமானதாக இருக்கும்.

வரிக் குறைப்பினால் அரசுக்கு ஏற்பட்ட வருவாய் இழப்புதான் தற்போதைய பொருளாதார நெருக்கடியின் தாய். நவீன பணவியல் கோட்பாட்டாளர்கள் என்று தங்களை அழைத்துக் கொள்ளும் பிரதான பொருளாதார நிபுணர்களிடமிருந்து பிரிந்த குழுவினால் முன்வைக்கப்பட்ட ஒரு சித்தாந்தத்தைப் பின்பற்றி, கோட்டாவின் கொள்கை ஆலோசகர்கள் வரவு செலவுத் திட்டப் பற்றாக்குறைக்கு நிதியளிப்பதற்காக வங்கித் துறையில் உருவாக்கப்பட்ட பணத்தை தாராளமாகப் பயன்படுத்தினர்.

இந்த அணுகுமுறையைப் பின்பற்றுவதன் ஆபத்தை இந்தியப் பொருளாதார நிபுணர் பி.ஆர். ஷெனாய் 1940களில் இலங்கைப் பல்கலைக்கழகத்தில் பொருளாதாரம் கற்பித்தவர். 2004 இல் 'கோட்பாட்டு பார்வை' என்ற தலைப்பில் வெளியிடப்பட்ட ஒரு புத்தகத்தில், அவர் தன்னார்வ சேமிப்பு மற்றும் விருப்பமில்லாத சேமிப்பு ஆகியவற்றுக்கு இடையேயான வேறுபாட்டைக் காட்டினார், தன்னார்வ சேமிப்பு என்பது உண்மையான நுகர்வைக் குறைப்பதன் மூலம் மக்கள் செய்யும் உண்மையான தியாகங்கள் மற்றும் உண்மையான முதலீடுகளில் பயன்படுத்த முதலீட்டாளர்களுக்குக் கிடைக்கும். உதாரணமாக, ஒரு மனிதன் 100 கிலோ அரிசியை உற்பத்தி செய்து, 80 கிலோவை மட்டுமே உட்கொண்டால், அவன் தன்னிச்சையாக 20 கிலோ அரிசியை உண்மையான முறையில் சேமிப்பான். இது முதலீட்டாளர்களுக்கு உற்பத்தி செய்யக் கிடைக்கிறது, ஷெனாய் கருத்துப்படி, பொருளாதார வல்லுநர்கள் முதலீட்டுப் பொருட்கள் என்று அழைக்கும் உயர் வரிசைப் பொருட்களைத் தயாரிக்கிறார்கள்.

இந்த முதலீட்டு பொருட்கள் எதிர்காலத்தில் அதிக நுகர்வு பொருட்களை உற்பத்தி செய்யும், மக்களின் உண்மையான வருமானத்தை அதிகரிக்கும், இதனால் அவர்கள் அதிக நுகர்வு பொருட்களை நுகரும். அதனால் பொருளாதாரக் கட்டமைப்பில் மாற்றம் ஏற்பட்டு அதன் மூலம் நீண்ட கால பொருளாதார செழுமையை உருவாக்குகிறது. தன்னிச்சையான சேமிப்பு என்பது மத்திய வங்கியால் அச்சிடப்பட்ட பணத்தின் ஆதரவுடன் வங்கிகளால் செயற்கையான கடன் மற்றும் வைப்புத்தொகையை உருவாக்குவதன் மூலம் உருவாக்கப்பட்ட சேமிப்பு ஆகும். இந்த சேமிப்புகள், உண்மையான நுகர்வைக் குறைப்பதன் மூலம் மக்கள் செய்யும் உண்மையான சேமிப்பிற்கு எதிராக நிதி நிறுவனங்களின் கற்பனைச் சொத்துக்களிலிருந்து எழும் கற்பனைச் சேமிப்புகள் ஆகும். ஷெனாய் கூறுகையில், முதலில் இதுபோன்ற கற்பனையான தன்னிச்சையான சேமிப்புகள் வெளியீட்டை அதிகரிக்கலாம் ஆனால் முதலீட்டுப் பொருட்களின் உண்மையான உற்பத்தியால் ஆதரிக்கப்படாததால் அதைத் தக்கவைக்க முடியாது. எனவே, இது பேரழிவிற்கு வழிவகுக்கிறது. அந்த அனர்த்தத்தை இலங்கை தற்போது அனுபவித்து வருகின்றது.

பணம் அச்சிடுவதன் மூலம், நுகர்வு பொருட்களுக்கான மொத்த தேவை அதிகரிக்கும். இந்த பொருட்களில் பெரும்பாலானவை வெளிநாட்டில் இருந்து பெறப்படுவதால், இது நாட்டின் அற்ப அந்நிய செலாவணி கையிருப்பில் இறக்குமதி செலவை அதிகரிக்கும். 2019 டிசம்பரில் இலங்கையில் 7.6 பில்லியன் டாலர் அந்நிய செலாவணி இருப்பு இருந்தது. இது 2022 மே மாத வாக்கில் மிகக் குறைந்த அளவிற்கு வீழ்ச்சியடைந்துள்ளது. மிக மோசமான நிலை என்னவென்றால், மத்திய வங்கியின் நிகர வெளிநாட்டு சொத்துக்கள் எதிர்மறையாகி, அவை $4.4 எதிர்மறை நிலுவையாக உள்ளது. மார்ச் 2022க்குள் பில்லியன். அந்நியச் செலாவணி கிடைக்காத நிலையில், மாற்று விகிதம் ஒரு இலவச வீழ்ச்சியில் உள்ளது மற்றும் ஒரு டாலருக்கு ரூ.360 என நிர்ணயிக்கும் மத்திய வங்கியின் முயற்சி முறையான வங்கி நிறுவனங்களில் வெளிநாட்டு நாணயங்களின் கடுமையான பற்றாக்குறையை உருவாக்கியுள்ளது. முறையான வங்கி நிறுவனங்களுக்கு வெளியே டாலர்களுக்கான இலாபகரமான கறுப்புச் சந்தையை உருவாக்குவது இதன் விளைவாகும்.

அந்நியச் செலாவணி இல்லாமல், இலங்கையால் எந்தக் கொடுப்பனவும் செய்ய முடியாது. வணிக மற்றும் இருதரப்பு கடன் வழங்குபவர்களுக்கான கட்டணம் ஏற்கனவே நிறுத்தப்பட்டுள்ளது. அத்தியாவசிய இறக்குமதிகளுக்கு பணம் செலுத்த வங்கி அமைப்பில் வெளிநாட்டு நாணயம் இல்லை. எரிபொருள், சமையல் எரிவாயு, மருந்துகள், உணவுகள் மற்றும் மூலப்பொருட்கள் போன்ற முதலீட்டு பொருட்கள் போன்ற நுகர்வு பொருட்கள் இதில் அடங்கும். இந்த தட்டுப்பாடு ஒரு பக்கம் வரிசையை உருவாக்கியுள்ளது. தொழில் துறைக்கு தேவையான மூலப்பொருட்களையும் பறித்துள்ளனர். இதன் விளைவாக, பொருளாதார நடவடிக்கைகள் தடைபட்டு வேலையில்லா திண்டாட்டம், வருமான இழப்பு மற்றும் நீடித்த மனச்சோர்வுக்கு வழிவகுத்தது. எனவே, திட்டமிடப்படாத வரிக் குறைப்புகளால் ஏற்படும் வருவாய் இழப்பு இந்த பொருளாதாரம் முழுவதும் ஏற்படும் நோய்களுக்கு முக்கிய பங்களிப்பு முகவராக உள்ளது.

இந்த பின்னணியில், RW இன் வரி உயர்வு வரவேற்கத்தக்கது. இது நிச்சயமாக மக்கள் மீது வரிச்சுமையை ஏற்றும். இருப்பினும், அரசாங்க செலவினங்களுக்கு நிதியளிப்பதற்காக அதிக பணத்தை அச்சிடுவதற்கான மாற்றானது பணவீக்க அழுத்தங்களால் அது கொண்டு வரும் மிகவும் வேதனையானதாக இருக்கும். ஏற்கனவே, உத்தியோகபூர்வ பணவீக்க விகிதம் 39% ஆக உள்ளது, உணவுப் பணவீக்கம் 57% ஆக உள்ளது. பட்ஜெட்டுக்கு நிதியளிக்க அரசாங்கம் பணம் அச்சிடுவதை நாடினால், இது மேலும் உயரும். எனவே, RW குறைந்த தீமையைத் தேர்ந்தெடுத்துள்ளது.

இருப்பினும், அவரது பொறுப்பு அதோடு நின்றுவிடக் கூடாது. பொருளாதாரத்தில் உண்மையான துறையை மேம்படுத்த அவர் ஒரு சாலை வரைபடத்தை கொண்டு வர வேண்டும். புதிய தொழில்நுட்பத்தை அறிமுகப்படுத்துவதற்கும் இலங்கையின் பொருளாதாரத்தை உலகப் பொருளாதாரத்துடன் மிகக் குறுகிய காலத்திற்குள் சீரமைப்பதற்குமான உத்திகள் அந்தச் சாலை வரைபடத்தில் அவசியமாக இருக்க வேண்டும். இது இல்லாமல், பட்ஜெட்டை மீட்பதற்காக வெறும் வரி உயர்வு பலனளிக்காது.

en.title: Ranil Wickremesinghe should come up with a road map to improve the real sector in the economy and introduce new technology and align Sri Lanka’s economy to the global economy within the shortest time possible.
body_lines: The Ministry of Finance, under the direction of Prime Minister and Finance Minister Ranil Wickremesinghe, known as RW, has hiked main taxes in the country moving halfway back to the tax regime that prevailed prior to 2020. Before this action, the Inland Revenue Services Union, made up of Assistant and Deputy Commissioners, had addressed a memo to RW suggesting measures to be taken to deliver an improved annual tax harvest of Rs. 1,200 billion. The tax measures introduced by the Finance Ministry are short of these proposals. Perhaps in the next round of tax reforms, the Ministry might reckon those proposals and if it does not do, its budgetary position will remain perilous.

The tax cuts offered by the Gotabaya Rajapaksa administration in January 2020 has been a contentious issue. The rationale presented by his policy advisors for these unsolicited tax concessions goes as follows. The previous tax regime introduced by Finance Minister Mangala Samaraweera had planned to attain fiscal consolidation – action taken to reduce the budget deficit to an affordable level and thereby check on debt accumulation – by increasing tax revenue. This is because the administration at that time had felt that attaining that objective by curtailing Government expenditure was considered an impractical strategy. But the administration had paid attention to rationalising Government expenditure as a means of improving the productivity of the sector. Accordingly, in the medium term, tax revenue was to be raised from 11.8% of GDP in 2018 to 15% by 2023.

However, such a high tax level was viewed as an unnecessary burden imposed on real economic activities by Gota’s policy advisors. Hence, instead of seeking to attain fiscal consolidation by increasing revenue, a strategy aiming at curtailing Government expenditure was planned. On the revenue side, it was planned to concentrate on a small number of large taxpayers and recover the tax loss by collecting the maximum from them. The rationale behind this strategy was that when tax rates are reduced, people would pay more taxes leading to an increase in the total tax revenue, a concept proposed by economist Arthur Laffer and now known as Laffer curve strategy.

When this policy was announced Gota’s election manifesto, it appeared to independent economic analysts that it was a recipe for disaster. I took up the issue in December 2019 when the new government was making plans for its introduction and warned in an article in this series that the Government should control the damage before the unconventional stimulus would backfire (available at: https://www.ft.lk/columns/Tax-cuts-Control-the-damage-before-the-unconventional-stimulus-backfires/4-691207). The following is a reproduction of some of the arguments made in this article.

The Cabinet spokesman Dr. Bandula Gunawardena had hailed the proposed tax cut that it will leave more cash in the hands of individuals and businesses enabling them to overcome the economic hardships they had been undergoing for some time. The public at large also welcomed it because they had more cash on hand to spend.

But the Moody’s Rating Services branded the stimulus as ‘credit negative’ implying that it would lead to a downgrading of the country’s present rating of B1/Stable unless it is managed properly. The Fitch rating had warned that the stimulus package which is hailed by Bandula Gunawardena will derail the budget disciplining exercise started by the previous government. A similar view was expressed by IMF’s Sri Lanka country head too. Dr. Indrajit Coomaraswamy who was about to leave the Central Bank at that time had warned that the stimulus package in question should not undermine the debt sustainability exercise introduced by the previous government.

The World Bank’s Chief Economist for South Asia, Hans Timmer, delivering a public lecture at the Central Bank’s Centre for Banking Studies was more polite in expressing his concerns. He said that though there may be reason for fiscal stimulus, there is no space for it in Sri Lanka and the policymakers should strike a proper balance between fiscal stimulus and the need for preventing the overheating of the economy – a situation where the aggregate demand was rising faster than the aggregate supply.

What it means is that if Sri Lanka’s potential growth is low, stimulating the economy either through fiscal policy or through monetary policy would simply increase the demand causing the prices to increase and the exchange rate to fall. Both these outcomes are what the Government wants to prevent from happening at any cost. Immediately, the reaction of the international community was visible in the form of a decline in the market prices of the international sovereign bonds issued by the Government.

I argued that the expectation of increasing revenue by cutting tax rates following the Laffer curve strategy was not relevant to Sri Lanka. Though the Laffer curve was a general law in economics, empirical studies have shown that it would be mostly effective when the tax rates are 70% or above. But Sri Lanka’s marginal tax rate was just 24% at that time and, hence, it had not reached the level at which the taxpayers would be incentivised to pay more taxes when the rates were cut.

Hence, it is not the Laffer curve which may have prompted Sri Lankan authorities to cut tax rates but some other consideration. That is to provide an additional income to the hands of the taxpayers, catch more liable people into the tax net and increase the tax revenue by getting more people to pay taxes. This last goal had been expressly mentioned in the election manifesto. According to press reports, the tax cut is intended to address both the individual taxpayers and the corporate taxpayers. The Government was therefore to lose a significant amount of tax revenue due to the tax cuts. That was estimated in December 2019 at about Rs. 650-680 billion. That was a revenue loss that could not be afforded by the cash-strapped Government. This advice as well as the warnings of others was not heeded to by Gota’s policy advisors. They went ahead with their program and implemented it.

When Sri Lanka was hit by the outbreak of the COVID-19 pandemic in the early part of 2020, due to the loss in revenue, the country had already lost the necessary fiscal space to address it. Expenditure had ballooned increasing the fiscal deficit to unaffordable levels thereby derailing the fiscal consolidation program initiated by the previous government. Given this predicament, in another article published in May 2020, I suggested to the Gotabaya administration that it should consider postponing the costly tax reforms (available at: https://www.ft.lk/columns/Constrained-fiscal-space-for-post-COVID-19-reconstruction-Consider-postponing-costly-tax-reforms/4-700327).

The Government was to use the Central Bank’s unelected money printing power to fill the budgetary gap and it entailed more problems than solutions. That was because when money was issued excessively, it would cause to establish inflationary pressures in the economy. Since the economy was also spiralling downward, it was obvious that it would also be hit by an economic depression. Hence, Gota’s liberal approach of using Central Bank’s money printing power was infecting Sri Lanka’s economy with a new ailment. That was inflationary depression. That ailment characterised by massive unemployment and low incomes amidst rising prices was to sow the seeds of an uncontrollable social and political disorder. Gota’s policy advisors who led the country to this disaster are no more with him. But the prediction made in May 2020 has now come true causing innumerable hardships to Sri Lankan people.

Hence, my suggestion to Gota was as follows: ‘These types of tax reforms should be tried under normal situations. The present COVID-19 environment is not the ideal situation to do so. It has handicapped the government by restricting its freedom to respond to the rising demands made of it by affected individuals and businesses. If the revenue does not flow into the Consolidated Fund, there is no way for the Minister of Finance to withdraw funds from it by issuing a warrant under the Constitution. Even after the expected approval of a formal budget by the new Parliament, the government will continue to suffer from this handicap due to its low revenue base. It will not be able to meet its financial obligations unless it borrows heavily from the local and foreign markets. Both would worsen the country’s debt situation pushing it further down to an inescapable debt trap. This has to be avoided at all costs. Given these circumstances, it will be prudent for the government to postpone its generous tax reforms until the country returns to normalcy’.

The loss in government revenue due to the tax cut has been the Mother of the Present Economic Crisis. Following an ideology presented by a breakaway group from mainstream economists who called themselves Modern Monetary Theorists, Gota’s policy advisors had liberally used the created moneys in the banking sector to finance the budget deficit.

The danger of following this approach has been presented by Indian economist B.R. Shenoy who taught economics in 1940s at the University of Ceylon. In a book published in 2004 under the title ‘Theoretical Vision’, he has made a distinction between voluntary savings and involuntary savings Voluntary savings are real sacrifices made by people by curtailing real consumption and made available for investors for use in real investments. For instance, if a man produces 100 kg of rice and consumes only 80 kg, he makes a voluntary saving of 20 kg of rice in real terms. This is made available to investors to produce, according to Shenoy, higher order goods which economists call investment goods.

These investment goods will produce more consumption goods in the future, increasing the real income of people thus facilitating them to consume more consumption goods. It therefore leads to a change in the economic structure and thereby generation of long-term economic prosperity. Involuntary savings are those savings created out of the artificial credit and deposit creation by banks with the support of money printed by the central bank. These savings are imaginary savings arising from imaginary assets of financial institutions as against the real savings made by people by curtailing real consumption. Shenoy says that at first such imaginary involuntary savings can increase output but cannot sustain it since they are not backed by real production of investment goods. Hence, it leads to disaster. Sri Lanka is presently experiencing that disaster.

With money printing, aggregate demand for consumption goods will increase. Since most of these goods are obtained from abroad, it will increase the import bill eating into the country’s meagre foreign exchange reserves. Sri Lanka had a foreign exchange balance of $ 7.6 billion in December 2019. This has fallen to a negligible level by May 2022. The worst situation has been that Central Bank’s net foreign assets have become negative and they amount to a negative balance of $ 4.4 billion by March 2022. With no foreign exchange available, exchange rate has been in a freefall and Central Bank’s attempt at fixing it at Rs 360 per dollar has created an acute shortage of foreign currencies in formal banking institutions. The corollary has been the development of a lucrative black market for dollars outside the formal banking institutions.

Without foreign exchange, Sri Lanka cannot make any payments. Payment to commercial and bilateral lenders has already been suspended. There is no foreign currency available in the banking system to pay for essential imports. That includes consumption goods like fuel, cooking gas, medicines, foods, and investment goods like raw materials. These shortages have created queues on one side. They have also deprived the industrial sector of necessary raw materials. As a result, As a result, economic activities have been hampered leading to unemployment, income losses, and prolonged depression. Hence, the loss of revenue due to unplanned tax cuts has been the main contributory agent for these economy-wide ailments.

In this background, RW’s tax hike is welcome. It will certainly pass a tax burden on to people. However, the alternative of printing more money to finance the Government expenditure will be more painful due to inflationary pressures that it will bring about. Already, the official inflation rate is running at 39% with a food inflation of 57%. This will further rise, if the Government resorts to money printing to finance the budget. Hence, RW has chosen the lesser evil.

However, his responsibility should not stop there. He should come up with a road map to improve the real sector in the economy. That road map should necessarily contain strategies to introduce new technology and align Sri Lanka’s economy to the global economy within the shortest time possible. Without this, a mere tax hike to rescue the budget will be fruitless.
